channel.title: World
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from World, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Trump remains ‘very serious’ about taking over Greenland, Danish PM warns – Europe live
item.description: ‘I think the desire from the US president is exactly the same,’ Mette Frederiksen tells Munich Security Conference Rubio insists that the US “do not s...

item.title: Brazil’s Pinheiro Braathen wins gold – and South America’s first Winter Olympics medal
item.description: Norwegian-born skier storms to historic slalom gold ‘Your difference is your superpower,’ says 25-year-old As the snow fell in Bormio, and the fog set...

item.title: US launches airstrikes on dozens of Islamic State targets in Syria
item.description: Militant group’s infrastructure and weapons storage facilities were hit, as Washington praised Damascus for fresh coalition role The US military condu...

item.title: Labubus to burkinis: V&A unveils updated 21st-century design galleries
item.description: Museum’s revitalised galleries bring together 250 objects to show how design shapes modern life What do the first ever baby monitor, Nigeria’s 2018 Wo...

item.title: ‘Nothing says love like chemicals’: Valentine’s roses often covered in pesticides, testing finds
item.description: Bouquets imported to Europe found to be heavily contaminated, often with chemicals banned in EU and UK Stay away from roses this Valentine’s Day, envi...

item.title: Skier makes history for Brazil by winning gold in giant slalom
item.description: Lucas Pinheiro Braathen makes history for Brazil by clinching his nation's first Winter Olympic medal with gold in the giant slalom.

item.title: One giant boys' club? Why Westminster can still feel like a man's world
item.description: The decision to appoint Peter Mandelson has prompted soul searching about women’s role in government, writes Laura Kuenssberg.

item.title: Russia killed opposition leader Alexei Navalny using dart frog toxin, UK says
item.description: There is no innocent explanation for the toxin being found in samples taken from Navalny's body, Foreign Office says.

item.title: Sweden survive crash chaos to win silver in cross-country relay
item.description: Sweden claim an incredible silver medal as Frida Karlsson completes a heroic comeback after Ebba Andersson crashed and lost one of her skis in the wom...

item.title: 'She's running with the ski under her arm' - Sweden survive crash chaos to win silver
item.description: Sweden claim an incredible silver medal as Frida Karlsson completes a heroic comeback after Ebba Andersson crashed and lost one of her skis in the wom...
